# Deep Vortex AI 3D Model Generator

> Transform any image into a 3D model using AI — download GLB files instantly, preview in-browser, import into Blender, Unity, or Unreal.

## URL

https://3d.deepvortexai.com

## About

The Deep Vortex AI 3D Model Generator converts a single 2D image into a fully realized 3D model using cutting-edge AI technology. Upload a photo of any object — a product, a character, an architectural element, a vehicle, a prop, or any subject — and receive a downloadable GLB file in seconds. No 3D modeling experience is required.

The generated GLB files are compatible with all major 3D software and game engines, including Blender, Unity, Unreal Engine, Maya, and Three.js. An in-browser 3D preview lets you rotate and inspect the model from all angles before downloading. This tool dramatically reduces the time and expertise required to go from a 2D reference to a 3D asset — a process that traditionally takes hours of skilled work in 3D software.

Whether you are a game developer generating prop assets, a product designer creating 3D visualizations, an AR/VR developer building immersive experiences, or a 3D printing enthusiast turning photos into printable models, the AI 3D Model Generator opens a new creative pipeline. New accounts receive 2 free credits to try it immediately.

## Features

- Image-to-3D model generation using advanced AI reconstruction
- GLB file output — the industry-standard format for 3D assets
- In-browser 3D preview with full rotation — inspect the model from every angle before downloading
- Supports JPG, PNG, and WEBP input image formats
- Instant processing — 3D models generated in seconds
- One-click GLB file download
- Compatible with Blender, Unity, Unreal Engine, Maya, Three.js, and all major 3D tools
- Suitable for 3D printing, AR/VR, game development, and product visualization
- Mobile-friendly interface for uploading and previewing models on any device
- Shared credit wallet integrated with all Deep Vortex AI tools

## Use Cases

- Generating 3D prop and environment assets for video games from 2D reference images
- Converting product photos into 3D models for AR try-on and product visualization experiences
- Creating 3D assets for AR/VR scenes and immersive experiences from photographic references
- Turning product images into 3D models for e-commerce 3D product viewers
- Generating base meshes for 3D artists to refine and detail in Blender or Maya
- Converting physical objects into 3D printable models from photos
- Rapid prototyping of 3D assets for game jams, demos, and pitches
- Creating 3D models for architectural visualization from reference imagery
- Producing 3D assets for motion graphics and visual effects work
- Generating character and creature base models from concept art or illustrations

## Target Audience

- Game developers who need 3D assets quickly without full 3D modeling workflows
- 3D artists looking for AI-generated base meshes to refine and detail
- AR and VR developers building immersive experiences that require custom 3D assets
- Product designers creating 3D visualizations and mockups from reference photos
- 3D printing enthusiasts who want to turn photographs of objects into printable models
- E-commerce businesses adding 3D product visualization to their online stores
- Architects and interior designers generating 3D models from reference images
- Indie game studios and game jam participants needing rapid asset generation
- Motion graphics artists building 3D-enhanced visual content
- Educators and students learning 3D workflows using AI-generated starting points

## Pricing

All credits are shared across every Deep Vortex AI tool. New accounts receive 2 free credits on sign-up — no payment required.

- Starter: 10 credits — $4.99
- Basic: 30 credits — $9.99
- Popular: 75 credits — $19.99
- Pro: 200 credits — $49.99
- Ultimate: 500 credits — $99.99

Credits do not expire. Use them on any tool in the Deep Vortex AI ecosystem at any time.

## FAQ

Q: What file format does the 3D Model Generator output?
A: The tool outputs GLB files — the binary version of the glTF format, which is the industry-standard format for 3D assets. GLB files are compatible with Blender, Unity, Unreal Engine, Maya, Three.js, and virtually all modern 3D software and game engines.

Q: Can I preview the 3D model before downloading?
A: Yes. The tool includes an in-browser 3D preview that renders the generated model and lets you rotate it in all directions, inspect it from every angle, and assess quality before downloading the GLB file.

Q: What kinds of images produce the best 3D models?
A: The best results come from images with a clearly visible subject, good lighting, and a relatively clean or simple background. Front-facing objects with visible depth cues — such as products on a surface, figures in a neutral environment, or objects photographed from a slight angle — tend to produce the most accurate 3D reconstructions.

Q: Is the GLB compatible with Blender?
A: Yes. Blender natively imports GLB/glTF files. Simply use File > Import > glTF 2.0 in Blender to load your generated model. From there it can be refined, textured, rigged, and used in any Blender workflow.

Q: Can I use the generated 3D models for 3D printing?
A: Yes. The GLB output can be converted to STL format — the standard for 3D printing — using free tools like Blender or online converters. The quality and printability of the model depends on the complexity of the source image and the subject matter.

Q: How much does one 3D model generation cost?
A: Each 3D model generation costs 1 credit from your shared Deep Vortex AI wallet. Credits work across all tools in the ecosystem — image generation, background removal, voice synthesis, video animation, and more.

Q: Does the generated 3D model include textures?
A: Yes. The AI generates the 3D model with textures derived from the input image, embedded in the GLB file. Textures can be further refined or replaced in Blender, Substance Painter, or any other 3D texturing tool.
